title: 40-class beta-range SSVEP speller dataset
description: A 40-class steady-state visually evoked potential (SSVEP) speller dataset acquired from 40 healthy participants across 6 sessions using beta-range frequency stimulation (14–21.8 Hz). The dataset comprises 31-channel EEG recordings sampled at 1024 Hz with joint frequency and phase modulation (JFPM) visual stimuli designed to reduce visual fatigue in brain-computer interface applications. This derivative dataset includes preprocessed, epoched data suitable for benchmarking classification algorithms including CCA, FBCCA, ITCCA, TRCA, and EEGNet.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a BioSemi ActiveTwo system with 31 active wet Ag/AgCl electrodes in a standard 10-05 montage, sampled at 1024 Hz with CMS/DRL reference and ground near Pz. Visual stimuli were presented using OpenViBE software with JFPM modulation across 40 frequency classes (14–21.8 Hz in 0.2 Hz steps). Each trial lasted 5 seconds with 6 blocks per session and 240 total trials per subject. Impedance was maintained below 5 kOhm.
